title: cordia caffra
intro: Cordia caffra, also known as the white sage or wild cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cordia caffra include treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as providing relief from pain and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
